semantic.role: This document defines the architecture, behavior, and protocol of ArchSpine, a mirror system that creates and maintains a synchronized, governance-aware representation of a software repository for both human and AI consumption.
responsibilities: Rule-based architecture enforcement (layered architecture, naming conventions)
AST extraction for multiple programming languages
Synchronization between source repository and mirrored artifacts
View generation (architecture diagrams, public surface, risk hotspots, project health)
Integration with LLM providers and MCP tools
AI agent briefing and change impact analysis
Sync, check, fix, scan, view, init, try, remove, config, info, rules, skill CLI commands
Documentation and atlas for multi-language support
outOfScope: Deployment pipelines and CI/CD orchestration outside of provided integrations
Direct modification of source code beyond suggested fixes
Network monitoring or external service availability

English.purpose: The purpose of this document is to serve as the authoritative narrative and technical reference for the ArchSpine mirror system. It explains why ArchSpine exists: to bridge the gap between raw codebases and structured, governance-aware representations that both humans and AI agents can navigate and use reliably. The document outlines the core concepts—spine, atlas, manifest, rules, views—and how they interact to maintain a living mirror of a repository's architecture and health.
English.context_and_audience: This document is intended for developers, architects, and AI agent operators who need to understand or contribute to the ArchSpine system. It is also designed for AI agents themselves, providing structured schemas and rules to allow automated tooling to consume, validate, and extend the mirrored representation. Teams adopting governance workflows, or those seeking to integrate AI assistance into their development process without losing architectural coherence, will find this document essential.
key_takeaways: ArchSpine maintains a synchronized mirror of a codebase, enabling both human understanding and AI-driven tooling.
The system enforces architecture rules (e.g., layered architecture, naming conventions) through scans and checkpoints.
AST extraction supports multiple programming languages, allowing language-specific rule application.
Views and briefings provide actionable insights such as risk hotspots, public surface analysis, and project health.
MCP integration allows AI agents to query and interact with the mirrored repository in real time.
The manifest and index ensure integrity and traceability of the mirrored state across versions.
